understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete each level by positioning every selectable piece onto the goal tile(s) marked with '+' on the grid, with the requirement that if a level had multiple goals they all had to be simultaneously occupied by selectables to trigger a WIN and progress through all levels.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions (UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT) attempted to shift the currently selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) in that direction; if the destination was blocked by a wall or an immovable boundary the piece would not move. Some adjacent objects (blocks) could be pushed if immediately next to the selected piece in the movement direction and if space allowed them to slide along with the move. Levels could contain multiple selectable pieces; control could be switched by CLICKing on another selectable’s exact pixel coordinate as listed in the state. The 'movement.*_blocked' flags were only hints about adjacency and didn’t guarantee success or failure.
